No. All campgrounds in the main park are closed and camping reservations aren't available there. Rancho del Oso, the park's coastal unit, is partially reopened with walk-in and bike-in camping options.

How far is Big Basin from Boulder Creek?

The main entrance is about 9 miles north of downtown Boulder Creek on Highway 236. The road is narrow and curvy, so plan on roughly half an hour rather than a quick hop.

Did the redwoods survive the CZU fire?

Mostly, yes. The 2020 fire affected 97 percent of the park and destroyed nearly all of its buildings, but State Parks reports that most of the old-growth redwoods survived and are regrowing. The forest looks different now, more open and sunlit, with green regrowth climbing blackened trunks.

What's the best short hike right now?

The Redwood Loop Trail, an easy 0.6-mile walk through the old-growth grove, is the reliable choice for a first visit. Longer options like the reopened stretch to Sempervirens Falls exist, but check the current trail map on the official park page, since individual trails open and close as recovery work continues.

title: Where to Eat in Boulder Creek Right Now
excerpt: If you're hungry in Boulder Creek today, you have real options: four sit-down restaurants, a taqueria and a Mexican counter spot, a hot dog stand with a fire pit, three cafes, three bakeries, two full markets and a deli, a bar, and a winery tasting room up the mountain.
bodyHtml:
If you're hungry in Boulder Creek today, you have real options: four sit-down restaurants, a taqueria and a Mexican counter spot, a hot dog stand with a fire pit, three cafes, three bakeries, two full markets and a deli, a bar, and a winery tasting room up the mountain. Every place on this list was confirmed operating on July 15, 2026, against its own website or a current official listing. That matters here, because search results for Boulder Creek dining still surface spots that closed years ago. This isn't a ranking and there's no "best" anything below, just what's actually open, grouped by type, with a link to each business's own page where one exists. Mountain-town hours change with the seasons, the weather, and the occasional staffing crunch, so check before you drive.

Sit-down restaurants

Scopazzi's Restaurant & Lounge at 13300 Big Basin Way is the town's historic special-occasion room, an Italian restaurant and lounge that's been feeding Boulder Creek for generations. It currently serves breakfast Wednesday through Sunday from 10:30 to 2, lunch Wednesday through Saturday, and dinner Wednesday through Sunday starting at 4, and it's closed Monday and Tuesday. There are private rooms if you're planning something bigger than dinner.

The Red Pearl at 13151 Highway 9 covers Chinese food for the whole valley, dine-in or takeout, Wednesday through Sunday 11 to 9. It's closed Monday and Tuesday, and the restaurant is clear on its site that it doesn't deliver, so plan on picking up.

Tae's Edo Sushi at 13271 Highway 9 is the sushi bar in town. Dinner runs 4 to 8 Tuesday through Thursday and Sunday, and Friday and Saturday add a lunch window from noon to 3 ahead of the usual dinner hours. Closed Monday.

The Sawmill Restaurant & Alehouse at 15520 Highway 9, a few minutes south of downtown, does burgers, pub fare, and craft beer with a big outdoor area and a music stage under the redwoods. It takes online orders through its site. Its posted hours vary across sources right now, so go straight to the website or call 831-610-8196 before heading over.

Counter service, tacos, and hot dogs

El Rey Leon at 12890 Central Avenue is a Mexican counter-service spot with online ordering. Posted hours are Monday through Saturday, 11 to 3 and again 4 to 7. Sunday isn't on the schedule the restaurant publishes, so call before a Sunday run.

Taqueria Los Gallos at 13070 Highway 9 downtown doesn't keep a website, but it's on the Boulder Creek Business Association's current food listings. Call 831-217-5000 for hours before you go.

River Dogs of Boulder Creek at 123 Forest Street, tucked behind Joe's Bar and Scarborough's, is an outdoor stand doing Chicago-style Vienna Beef hot dogs and sausages, with a fire pit and a small stage that hosts acoustic music. Its posted winter schedule is Tuesday through Thursday 11:30 to 3 and Friday through Sunday 11:30 to 5, and the stand closes for bad weather and special events, which is exactly what you'd expect from a place that's genuinely outdoors.

Coffee, breakfast, and lunch

Three cafes cover the morning shift, and all three are the kind of small operations where calling ahead beats trusting the internet.

Jenna Sue's Cafe at 13090 Highway 9 is the downtown coffeehouse, pouring Java Bob's coffee alongside breakfast, lunch, and baked goods. Its own page is older, but the business association lists it as current at 831-703-4280; confirm hours by phone.

Bella's Cafe at 13132 Central Avenue serves American breakfast and lunch. Its Visit Santa Cruz County listing shows daily 8 to 2, though that listing predates this year, so treat the hours as a starting point and call 831-217-5070 to be sure.

The Tree House Cafe at 13266 Highway 9 sits creekside at the north end of the business district with coffee, breakfast, and sandwiches. It runs on Facebook rather than a website; the business association listing has it current at 831-610-8912.

Bakeries

Dessert First Bakery at 13090 Highway 9, Suite 2, bakes custom goods with all-natural, locally sourced ingredients. Its Visit Santa Cruz County listing, updated in February 2026, shows daily hours of 6:30 a.m. to 1:30 p.m., which makes it the earliest food in town on paper. Custom work is the core of the business, so call 831-338-7008 before counting on a walk-in case.

Butter Late Than Never Baking Co. is a licensed cottage bakery working out of a Boulder Creek home kitchen, which means online orders rather than a storefront, and no published address. Cookies, brownies, mini donuts, and cookie cakes, with seasonal flavors rotating through the shop page, local delivery, and California-only shipping under cottage food rules.

Wildstone Bakery is Boulder Creek's naturally fermented bread and pastry maker, also without a storefront. You'll find its loaves, bagels, and seasonal tarts at the Downtown Santa Cruz Farmers Market on Wednesdays from 12:30 to 5, and at the Ben Lomond Village Market on first Saturdays, June through October 2026, from 10 to 2.

Markets and delis

Johnnie's Super Market at 13225 Highway 9 has been the town grocery since 1938, with produce, meat and seafood, a deli making sandwiches from family recipes, a bakery case, cheese, and a deep beer, wine, and spirits selection. Hours are Monday through Thursday and Sunday 8 to 9, Friday and Saturday 8 to 10, which makes it the latest food option in town most nights.

Wild Roots Market at 13159 Central Avenue is the natural grocery, open daily 9 to 9, with a deli kitchen turning out prepared foods plus produce, meat, and seafood sourced heavily from local farms.

The Redwood Keg at 12980 Highway 9 is the local liquor store with a deli counter, listed on the SLV Chamber's current food and takeout page. There's no website, so call 831-338-2727 to check deli hours before making the trip for a sandwich.

A bar and a winery

Joe's Bar at 13118 Highway 9 is the downtown bar and live music room, the sort of place locals will tell you defines the town. It appears on Visit Santa Cruz County's current dining pages, and its listing shows daily hours from 10 a.m., though that listing hasn't been refreshed recently, so confirm by phone at 831-338-9417 if timing matters.

Big Basin Vineyards' estate tasting room at 830 Memory Lane pours its mountain-grown wines at the vineyard itself on Saturdays and Sundays from noon to 5. Reservations go through the booking system on its site, and it's worth noting this is the Boulder Creek estate, separate from the winery's Santa Cruz tasting room.

Frequently Asked Questions

What's open on Monday in Boulder Creek?

Monday is the quiet day. Scopazzi's, the Red Pearl, and Tae's Edo are all closed, and River Dogs' winter schedule starts Tuesday. Your reliable Monday options are the markets, with Johnnie's and Wild Roots both open daily, plus El Rey Leon's posted Monday hours.

Which restaurants deliver?

Plan on pickup. The Red Pearl states plainly that it doesn't deliver, and El Rey Leon and the Sawmill run online ordering for pickup rather than delivery. The exception is baked goods: Butter Late Than Never delivers its cottage bakery orders locally.

Where can I eat early in the morning?

Dessert First's listing shows a 6:30 a.m. open, the earliest posted time in town. Bella's listing shows 8 a.m., and Johnnie's opens at 8 daily if a deli sandwich counts as breakfast. Call the cafes to confirm their current opening times.

Why aren't hours printed for every place on this list?

Because we couldn't verify them to a current source, and a wrong hour is worse than no hour. Where a business's posted schedule conflicted across sources or hadn't been updated recently, we left it out and gave you the phone number instead.

A place I know is missing. Why?

This list only includes businesses we could confirm operating on July 15, 2026, through their own site or a current official listing. A few local food makers without any public web presence didn't make the cut yet, and we'll add them as soon as they have a page we can point to. If you run one of them, get in touch.
